Terminology_code.name: Terminology_code
Terminology_code.documentation: Logically primitive type representing a reference to a terminology concept, in the form of a terminology identifier, optional version, and a code or code string from the terminology.

terminology_id.name: terminology_id
terminology_id.documentation: The archetype environment namespace identifier used to identify a terminology. Typically a value like "snomed_ct" that is mapped elsewhere to the full URI identifying the terminology.

terminology_version.name: terminology_version
terminology_version.documentation: Optional string value representing terminology version, typically a date or dotted numeric.

code_string.name: code_string
code_string.documentation: A terminology code or post-coordinated code expression, if supported by the terminology. The code may refer to a single term, a value set consisting of multiple terms, or some other entity representable within the terminology.

uri.name: uri
uri.documentation: The URI reference that may be used as a concrete key into a notional terminology service for queries that can obtain the term text, definition, and other associated elements.

Terminology_term.name: Terminology_term
Terminology_term.documentation: Leaf type representing a term from a terminology, which consists of the term text and the code, i.e. a concept reference.

concept.name: concept
concept.documentation: Refrence to the terminology concept formally representing this term.

Any.name: Any
Any.documentation: Abstract supertype. Usually maps to a type like Any or Object in an object-oriented technology. Defined here to provide value and reference equality semantics.

instance_of.name: instance_of
instance_of.documentation: Create new instance of a type.

type_of.name: type_of
type_of.documentation: Type name of an object as a string. May include generic parameters, as in "Interval<Time>".

Temporal.name: Temporal
Temporal.documentation: Abstract ancestor type of Date/Time types.

Date.name: Date
Date.documentation: Assumed built-in primitive type representing a date.

value.name: value
value.documentation: Internal private data value of a native Date. No assumption is made about either the naming of this attribute or its type, or if more than one attribute is used.

Date_time.name: Date_time
Date_time.documentation: Assumed built-in primitive type representing a date/time.

value.name: value
value.documentation: Internal private data value of a native Date/Time. No assumption is made about either the naming of this attribute or its type, or if more than one attribute is used.

Time.name: Time
Time.documentation: Assumed built-in primitive type representing a time.

value.name: value
value.documentation: Internal private data value of a native Time. No assumption is made about either the naming of this attribute or its type, or if more than one attribute is used.

Duration.name: Duration
Duration.documentation: Assumed built-in primitive type representing a duration.

value.name: value
value.documentation: Internal private data value of a native Duration. No assumption is made about either the naming of this attribute or its type, or if more than one attribute is used.

Hash.name: Hash
Hash.documentation: Type representing a keyed table of values. T is the value type, and U the type of the keys.

has_key.name: has_key
has_key.documentation: Test for membership of a key.

List.name: List
List.documentation: Ordered container that may contain duplicates.

Set.name: Set
Set.documentation: Unordered container that may not contain duplicates.

Array.name: Array
Array.documentation: Container whose storage is assumed to be contiguous.

Ordered_Numeric.name: Ordered_Numeric
Ordered_Numeric.documentation: Abstract notional parent class of ordered, numeric types, which are types with ‘<‘ and arithmetic operators defined.

Integer.name: Integer
Integer.documentation: Class representing minimal interface of standard Integer type.

Numeric.name: Numeric
Numeric.documentation: Abstract notional parent class of numeric types, which are types which have various arithmetic and comparison operators defined.

infix *.name: infix *
infix *.documentation: Product by `other'. Actual type of result depends on arithmetic balancing rules.

infix +.name: infix +
infix +.documentation: Sum with `other' (commutative). Actual type of result depends on arithmetic balancing rules.

infix -.name: infix -
infix -.documentation: Result of subtracting `other'. Actual type of result depends on arithmetic balancing rules.

Boolean.name: Boolean
Boolean.documentation: Class representing minimal interface of standard Boolean type.

post_conditions.Post_de_Morgan: Result = not (not self or not other)
Result = not (not self or else not other)
Result = not (not self and not other)
Result = not (not self and then not other)

post_conditions.Post_definition: Result = ((self or other) and not (self and other))

String.name: String
String.documentation: Strings of characters, as used to represent textual data in any natural or formal language.

infix +.name: infix +
infix +.documentation: Concatenation operator - causes ‘other’ to be appended to this string.

is_empty.name: is_empty
is_empty.documentation: True if string is empty, i.e. equal to "".

is_integer.name: is_integer
is_integer.documentation: True if string can be parsed as an integer.

as_integer.name: as_integer
as_integer.documentation: Return the integer corresponding to the integer value represented in this string.

Real.name: Real
Real.documentation: Type used to represent decimal numbers. Corresponds to a single-precision floating point value in most languages.

floor.name: floor
floor.documentation: Return the greatest integer no greater than the value of this object.

Integer64.name: Integer64
Integer64.documentation: Class representing minimal interface of standard Integer64 type.

Ordered.name: Ordered
Ordered.documentation: Abstract notional parent class of ordered, types i.e. types on which the ‘<‘ operator is defined.

infix <.name: infix <
infix <.documentation: Arithmetic comparison. In conjunction with ‘=’, enables the definition of the operators ‘>’, ‘>=’, ‘<=’, ‘<>’. In real type systems, this operator might be defined on another class for comparability.

Iso8601_date_time.name: Iso8601_date_time
Iso8601_date_time.documentation:
Represents an ISO 8601 date/time, including partial and extended forms.

Note that this class includes 2 deviations from ISO 8601:2004:

* for partial date/times, any part of the date/time up to the month may be missing, not just seconds and minutes as in the standard;
* the time 24:00:00 is not allowed, since it would mean the date was really on the next day.

month_unknown.name: month_unknown
month_unknown.documentation: Indicates whether month in year is unknown.

day_unknown.name: day_unknown
day_unknown.documentation: Indicates whether day in month is unknown.

minute_unknown.name: minute_unknown
minute_unknown.documentation: Indicates whether minute in hour is known.

second_unknown.name: second_unknown
second_unknown.documentation: Indicates whether minute in hour is known.

is_decimal_sign_comma.name: is_decimal_sign_comma
is_decimal_sign_comma.documentation: True if this time has a decimal part indicated by ‘,’ (comma) rather than ‘.’ (period).

valid_iso8601_date_time.name: valid_iso8601_date_time
valid_iso8601_date_time.documentation:
String is a valid ISO 8601 date-time, i.e. takes the form:

* YYYYMMDDThhmmss[(,|.)s+][Z|±hh[mm]]

or the extended form:

* YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss[(,|.)s+][Z|±hh[:mm]]

or one of the partial forms:

* YYYYMMDDThhmm
* YYYYMMDDThh

or the equivalent extended forms:

* YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm
* YYYY-MM-DDThh

(meanings as in DV_DATE, DV_TIME) and the values in each field are valid.

is_partial.name: is_partial
is_partial.documentation: True if this date time is partial, i.e. if seconds or more is missing.

is_extended.name: is_extended
is_extended.documentation: True if this date/time uses ‘-’, ‘:’ separators.

as_string.name: as_string
as_string.documentation:
ISO8601 string for date/time, in format:

* `YYYYMMDDThhmmss[,sss][Z | ±hh[mm]]` 

or in extended format:

* `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss[,sss][Z | ±hh[mm]]`

or a partial variant; see `_valid_iso8601_date_time()_` for validity.

has_fractional_second.name: has_fractional_second
has_fractional_second.documentation: True if the fractional_second part is signficant (i.e. even if = 0.0).

Iso8601_duration.name: Iso8601_duration
Iso8601_duration.documentation: Represents an ISO 8601 duration, which may have multiple parts from years down to seconds.

is_decimal_sign_comma.name: is_decimal_sign_comma
is_decimal_sign_comma.documentation: True if this time has a decimal part indicated by ',' (comma) rather than '.' (period).

valid_iso8601_duration.name: valid_iso8601_duration
valid_iso8601_duration.documentation:
String is a valid ISO 8601 duration, i.e. takes the form:

* P[nnY][nnM][nnW][nnD][T[nnH][nnM][nnS]]

Where each nn represents a number of years, months, etc. nnW represents a number of 7-day weeks.

Note: allowing the W designator in the same expression as other designators is an exception to the published standard, but necessary in clinical information (typically for representing pregnancy duration).

s.name: s
s.documentation:
String is a valid ISO 8601 duration, i.e. takes the form:

* P[nnY][nnM][nnW][nnD][T[nnH][nnM][nnS]]

Where each nn represents a number of years, months, etc. nnW represents a number of 7- day weeks.

NOTE: allowing the W designator in the same expression as other designators is an exception to the published standard, but necessary in clinical information (typically for representing pregnancy duration).

as_string.name: as_string
as_string.documentation:
ISO8601 string for duration, in format

* `P[nnY][nnM][nnW][nnD][T[nnH][nnM][nnS]]`

years.name: years
years.documentation: Number of years of nominal 365-day length.

to_seconds.name: to_seconds
to_seconds.documentation: Total number of seconds equivalent (including fractional) of entire duration.

Iso8601_time.name: Iso8601_time
Iso8601_time.documentation:
Represents an ISO 8601 time, including partial and extended forms.

NOTE: A small deviation to the ISO 8601:2004 standard in this class is that the time 24:00:00 is not allowed, for consistency with ISO8601_DATE_TIME.

minute_unknown.name: minute_unknown
minute_unknown.documentation: Indicates whether minute is unknown. If so, the time is of the form “hh”.

second_unknown.name: second_unknown
second_unknown.documentation: Indicates whether second is unknown. If so and month is known, the time is of the form “hh:mm” or “hhmm”.

is_decimal_sign_comma.name: is_decimal_sign_comma
is_decimal_sign_comma.documentation: True if this time has a decimal part indicated by ‘,’ (comma) rather than ‘.’ (period).

valid_iso8601_time.name: valid_iso8601_time
valid_iso8601_time.documentation:
String is a valid ISO 8601 date, i.e. takes the form:

* hhmmss[(,|.)s+][Z|±hh[mm]]

or the extended form:

* hh:mm:ss[(,|.)s+][Z|±hh[:mm]]

or one of the partial forms:

* hhmm or hh 

or the extended form:

* hh:mm

with an additional optional timezone indicator of:

* Z or ±hh[mm] or ±hh[:mm] (extended form)

Where:

* hh is “00” - “23” (0-filled to two digits)
* mm is “00” - “59” (0-filled to two digits)
* ss is “00” - “60” (0-filled to two digits)
* [(,|.)s+] is an optional string consisting of a comma or decimal point followed by numeric string of 1 or more digits, representing a fractional second
* Z is a literal meaning UTC (modern replacement for GMT), i.e. timezone +0000

is_partial.name: is_partial
is_partial.documentation: True if this time is partial, i.e. if seconds or more is missing.

is_extended.name: is_extended
is_extended.documentation: True if this time uses ‘-’, ‘:’ separators.

as_string.name: as_string
as_string.documentation:
ISO8601 string for time, i.e. in the form:

* `hhmmss[,sss][Z|±hh[mm]]`

or the extended form: 

* `hh:mm:ss[,sss][Z|±hh[mm]]`

or a partial invariant. See `_valid_iso8601_time()_` for validity.

has_fractional_second.name: has_fractional_second
has_fractional_second.documentation: True if the fractional_second part is signficant (i.e. even if = 0.0).

Iso8601_type.name: Iso8601_type
Iso8601_type.documentation: Abstract ancestor type of ISO 8601 types, defining interface for 'extended' and 'partial' concepts from ISO 8601.

is_partial.name: is_partial
is_partial.documentation: True if this date time is partial, i.e. if trailing end (right hand) value(s) is/are missing.

is_extended.name: is_extended
is_extended.documentation: True if this ISO8601 string is in the 'extended' form, i.e. uses ‘-’ and / or ‘:’ separators.

Iso8601_date.name: Iso8601_date
Iso8601_date.documentation: Represents an ISO 8601 date, including partial and extended forms.

month_unknown.name: month_unknown
month_unknown.documentation: Indicates whether month in year is unknown. If so, the date is of the form “YYYY”.

day_unknown.name: day_unknown
day_unknown.documentation: Indicates whether day in month is unknown. If so, and month is known, the date is of the form “YYYY-MM” or “YYYYMM”.

valid_iso8601_date.name: valid_iso8601_date
valid_iso8601_date.documentation:
String is a valid ISO 8601 date, i.e. takes the complete form:

* YYYYMMDD or the extended form:
* YYYY-MM-DD or one of the partial forms:
* YYYYMM
* YYYY

or the equivalent extended form:

* YYYY-MM

Where:

* YYYY is the string form of any positive number in the range “0000” - “9999” (zero-filled to four digits)
* MM is “01” - “12” (zero-filled to two digits)
* DD is “01” - “31” (zero-filled to two digits)

The combinations of YYYY, MM, DD numbers must be correct with respect to the Gregorian calendar.

is_partial.name: is_partial
is_partial.documentation: True if this date is partial, i.e. if days or more is missing.

is_extended.name: is_extended
is_extended.documentation: True if this date uses `'-'` separators.

as_string.name: as_string
as_string.documentation: ISO8601 string for date, in format `YYYYMMDD` or `YYYY-MM-DD`, or a partial invariant. See `_valid_iso8601_date()_` for validity.

invariants.Day_valid: not day_unknown implies valid_day (year, month, day)

Multiplicity_interval.name: Multiplicity_interval
Multiplicity_interval.documentation: An Interval of Integer, used to represent multiplicity, cardinality and optionality in models.

Multiplicity_range_marker.name: Multiplicity_range_marker
Multiplicity_range_marker.documentation: Marker to use in string form of interval between limits.

Multiplicity_unbounded_marker.name: Multiplicity_unbounded_marker
Multiplicity_unbounded_marker.documentation: Symbol to use to indicate upper limit unbounded.

is_open.name: is_open
is_open.documentation: True if this interval imposes no constraints, i.e. is set to 0..*

is_optional.name: is_optional
is_optional.documentation: True if this interval expresses optionality, i.e. 0..1.

is_mandatory.name: is_mandatory
is_mandatory.documentation: True if this interval expresses mandation, i.e. 1..1.

is_prohibited.name: is_prohibited
is_prohibited.documentation: True if this interval is set to 0..0.

Interval.name: Interval
Interval.documentation: Interval abstraction, featuring upper and lower limits that may be open or closed, included or not included.

lower_included.name: lower_included
lower_included.documentation: lower boundary value included in range if not lower_unbounded.

upper_included.name: upper_included
upper_included.documentation: upper boundary value included in range if not upper_unbounded.

has.name: has
has.documentation:
True if (lower_unbounded or 
((lower_included and v >= lower) or 
v > lower)) and 
(upper_unbounded or 
((upper_included and v <= upper or v 
< upper)))

intersects.name: intersects
intersects.documentation: True if there is any overlap between intervals represented by Current and `other'. True if at least one limit of other is strictly inside the limits of this interval.

contains.name: contains
contains.documentation: True if current interval properly contains `other'? True if all points of `other' are inside the current interval.

is_equal.name: is_equal
is_equal.documentation: True if current object's interval is semantically same as `other'.

invariants.Limits_consistent: (not upper_unbounded and not lower_unbounded) implies lower <= upper
invariants.Limits_comparable: (not upper_unbounded and not lower_unbounded) implies lower.strictly_comparable_to (upper)

Cardinality.name: Cardinality
Cardinality.documentation: Express constraints on the cardinality of container objects which are the values of multiply-valued attributes, including uniqueness and ordering, providing the means to state that a container acts like a logical list, set or bag. The cardinality cannot contradict the cardinality of the corresponding attribute within the relevant reference model.

is_ordered.name: is_ordered
is_ordered.documentation: True if the members of the container attribute to which this cardinality refers are ordered.

is_unique.name: is_unique
is_unique.documentation: True if the members of the container attribute to which this cardinality refers are unique.

is_bag.name: is_bag
is_bag.documentation: True if the semantics of this cardinality represent a bag, i.e. unordered, non-unique membership.

is_list.name: is_list
is_list.documentation: True if the semantics of this cardinality represent a list, i.e. ordered, non-unique membership.

is_set.name: is_set
is_set.documentation: True if the semantics of this cardinality represent a set, i.e. unordered, unique membership.

ARCHETYPE_ID.name: ARCHETYPE_ID
ARCHETYPE_ID.documentation:
Identifier for archetypes. Ideally these would identify globally unique archetypes.
Lexical form: `rm_originator  '-' rm_name  '-' rm_entity  '.' concept_name {  '-' specialisation }*  '.v' number`.

qualified_rm_entity.name: qualified_rm_entity
qualified_rm_entity.documentation: Globally qualified reference model entity, e.g.  openehr-composition-OBSERVATION.

domain_concept.name: domain_concept
domain_concept.documentation:
Name of the concept represented by this archetype, including specialisation, e.g. 
 Biochemistry_result-cholesterol .

rm_originator.name: rm_originator
rm_originator.documentation:
Organisation originating the reference model on which this archetype is based, e.g. 
 openehr ,  cen ,  hl7 .

rm_name.name: rm_name
rm_name.documentation: Name of the reference model, e.g. rim,  ehr_rm,  en13606 .

rm_entity.name: rm_entity
rm_entity.documentation: Name of the ontological level within the reference model to which this archetype is targeted, e.g. for openEHR,  folder ,  composition ,  section ,  entry .

specialisation.name: specialisation
specialisation.documentation: Name of specialisation of concept, if this archetype is a specialisation of another archetype, e.g.  cholesterol .

GENERIC_ID.name: GENERIC_ID
GENERIC_ID.documentation: Generic identifier type for identifiers whose format is otherwise unknown to openEHR. Includes an attribute for naming the identification scheme (which may well be local).

scheme.name: scheme
scheme.documentation: Name of the scheme to which this identifier conforms. Ideally this name will be recognisable globally but realistically it may be a local ad hoc scheme whose name is not controlled or standardised in any way.

HIER_OBJECT_ID.name: HIER_OBJECT_ID
HIER_OBJECT_ID.documentation: Concrete type corresponding to hierarchical identifiers of the form defined by UID_BASED_ID.

LOCATABLE_REF.name: LOCATABLE_REF
LOCATABLE_REF.documentation: Purpose Reference to a LOCATABLE instance inside the top-level content structure inside a VERSION<T>; the path attribute is applied to the object that VERSION.data points to.

path.name: path
path.documentation: The path to an instance in question, as an absolute path with respect to the object found at VERSION.data. An empty path means that the object referred to by id being specified.

as_uri.name: as_uri
as_uri.documentation: A URI form of the reference, created by concatenating the following: `ehr://  + id.value +  /  + path`.

OBJECT_ID.name: OBJECT_ID
OBJECT_ID.documentation:
Ancestor class of identifiers of informational objects. Ids may be completely meaningless, in which case their only job is to refer to something, or may carry some information to do with the identified object. 

Object ids are used inside an object to identify that object. To identify another object in another service, use an OBJECT_REF, or else use a UID for local objects identified by UID. If none of the subtypes is suitable, direct instances of this class may be used.

value.name: value
value.documentation: The value of the id in the form defined below.

OBJECT_REF.name: OBJECT_REF
OBJECT_REF.documentation: Class describing a reference to another object, which may exist locally or be maintained outside the current namespace, e.g. in another service. Services are usually external, e.g. available in a LAN (including on the same host) or the internet via Corba, SOAP, or some other distributed protocol. However, in small systems they may be part of the same executable as the data containing the Id.

namespace.name: namespace
namespace.documentation:
Namespace to which this identifier belongs in the local system context (and possibly in any other openEHR compliant environment) e.g.  terminology ,  demographic . These names are not yet standardised. Legal values for the namespace are:

* local
* unknown
* [a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_-:/&+?]*

type.name: type
type.documentation: Name of the  class (concrete or abstract) of object to which this identifier type refers, e.g.  PARTY ,  PERSON ,  GUIDELINE  etc. These class names are from the relevant reference model. The type name  ANY  can be used to indicate that any type is accepted (e.g. if the type is unknown).

id.name: id
id.documentation: Globally unique id of an object, regardless of where it is stored.

OBJECT_VERSION_ID.name: OBJECT_VERSION_ID
OBJECT_VERSION_ID.documentation: Globally unique identifier for one version of a versioned object; lexical form: `object_id  '::' creating_system_id  '::' version_tree_id`.

object_id.name: object_id
object_id.documentation: Unique identifier for logical object of which this identifier identifies one version; normally the object_id will be the unique identifier of the version container containing the version referred to by this OBJECT_VERSION_ID instance.

version_tree_id.name: version_tree_id
version_tree_id.documentation: Tree identifier of this version with respect to other versions in the same version tree, as either 1 or 3 part dot-separated numbers, e.g.  1 ,  2.1.4 .

creating_system_id.name: creating_system_id
creating_system_id.documentation: Identifier of the system that created the Version corresponding to this Object version id.

is_branch.name: is_branch
is_branch.documentation: True if this version identifier represents a branch.

PARTY_REF.name: PARTY_REF
PARTY_REF.documentation: Identifier for parties in a demographic or identity service. There are typically a number of subtypes of the PARTY class, including PERSON, ORGANISATION, etc. Abstract supertypes are allowed if the referenced object is of a type not known by the current implementation of this class (in other words, if the demographic model is changed by the addition of a new PARTY or ACTOR subtypes, valid PARTY_REFs can still be constructed to them).

invariants.Type_validity: type.is_equal(“PERSON”) or type.is_equal(“ORGANISATION”) or type.is_equal(“GROUP”) or type.is_equal(“AGENT”)or type.is_equal(“ROLE”) or type.is_equal(“PARTY”) or type.is_equal(“ACTOR”)

TERMINOLOGY_ID.name: TERMINOLOGY_ID
TERMINOLOGY_ID.documentation:
Identifier for terminologies such as accessed via a terminology query service. In this class, the value attribute identifies the Terminology in the terminology service, e.g.  SNOMED-CT . A terminology is assumed to be in a particular language, which must be explicitly specified.

The value if the id attribute is the precise terminology id identifier, including actual release (i.e. actual  version), local modifications etc; e.g. ICPC2.

Lexical form: `name [  (' version  )' ]`.

name.name: name
name.documentation: Return the terminology id (which includes the  version  in some cases). Distinct names correspond to distinct (i.e. non-compatible) terminologies. Thus the names  ICD10AM  and  ICD10  refer to distinct terminologies.

version_id.name: version_id
version_id.documentation: Version of this terminology, if versioning supported, else the empty string.

VERSION_TREE_ID.name: VERSION_TREE_ID
VERSION_TREE_ID.documentation:
Version tree identifier for one version. Lexical form: 

`trunk_version [  '.' branch_number  '.' branch_version ]`

trunk_version.name: trunk_version
trunk_version.documentation: Trunk version number; numbering starts at 1.

is_branch.name: is_branch
is_branch.documentation: True if this version identifier represents a branch, i.e. has branch_number and branch_version parts.

branch_number.name: branch_number
branch_number.documentation: Number of branch from the trunk point; numbering starts at 1.

branch_version.name: branch_version
branch_version.documentation: Version of the branch; numbering starts at 1.

invariants.Trunk_version_valid: trunk_version /= Void and then trunk_version.is_integer and then trunk_version.as_integer >= 1
invariants.Branch_number_valid: branch_number /= Void implies branch_number.is_integer and then branch_number.as_integer >= 1
invariants.Branch_version_valid: branch_version /= Void implies branch_version.is_integer and then branch_version.as_integer >= 1
invariants.Branch_validity: (branch_number = Void and branch_version = Void ) xor (branch_number /= Void and branch_version /= Void )

UID_BASED_ID.name: UID_BASED_ID
UID_BASED_ID.documentation:
Abstract model of UID-based identifiers consisting of a root part and an optional extension;

lexical form: `root '::' extension`.

root.name: root
root.documentation: The identifier of the conceptual namespace in which the object exists, within the identification scheme. Returns the part to the left of the first '::' separator, if any, or else the whole string.

extension.name: extension
extension.documentation: Optional local identifier of the object within the context of the root identifier. Returns the part to the right of the first  ::' separator if any, or else any empty String.

UUID.name: UUID
UUID.documentation: Model of the DCE Universal Unique Identifier or UUID which takes the form of hexadecimal integers separated by hyphens, following the pattern 8-4-4-4-12 as defined by the Open Group, CDE 1.1 Remote Procedure Call specification, Appendix A. Also known as a GUID.

INTERNET_ID.name: INTERNET_ID
INTERNET_ID.documentation: Model of a reverse internet domain, as used to uniquely identify an internet domain. In the form of a dot-separated string in the reverse order of a domain name, specified by IETF RFC 1034 (http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc1034.txt).

UID.name: UID
UID.documentation: Abstract parent of classes representing unique identifiers which identify information entities in a durable way. UIDs only ever identify one IE in time or space and are never re-used.

ISO_OID.name: ISO_OID
ISO_OID.documentation: Model of ISO's Object Identifier (oid) as defined by the standard ISO/IEC 8824. Oids are formed from integers separated by dots. Each non-leaf node in an Oid starting from the left corresponds to an assigning authority, and identifies that authority's namespace, inside which the remaining part of the identifier is locally unique.

TEMPLATE_ID.name: TEMPLATE_ID
TEMPLATE_ID.documentation: Identifier for templates. Lexical form to be determined.

ACCESS_GROUP_REF.name: ACCESS_GROUP_REF
ACCESS_GROUP_REF.documentation: Reference to access group in an access control service.

OPENEHR_DEFINITIONS.name: OPENEHR_DEFINITIONS
OPENEHR_DEFINITIONS.documentation: Inheritance class to provide access to constants defined in other packages.

Local_terminology_id.name: Local_terminology_id
Local_terminology_id.documentation: Predefined terminology identifier to indicate it is local to the knowledge resource in which it occurs, e.g. an archetype

VALIDITY_KIND.name: VALIDITY_KIND
VALIDITY_KIND.documentation:
An enumeration of three values that may commonly occur in constraint models.

Use as the type of any attribute within this model, which expresses constraint on some attribute in a class in a reference model. For example to indicate validity
of Date/Time fields.
item_documentations: Constant to indicate mandatory presence of something.
Constant to indicate optional presence of something.
Constant to indicate disallowed presence of something.

VERSION_STATUS.name: VERSION_STATUS
VERSION_STATUS.documentation: Status of a versioned artefact, as one of a number of possible values: uncontrolled, prerelease, release, build.
item_documentations: Value representing a version which is 'unstable', i.e. contains an unknown size of change with respect to its base version. Rendered with the build number as a string in the form `N.M.P-alpha.B` e.g. `2.0.1-alpha.154`.

Value representing a version which is 'beta', i.e. contains an unknown but reducing size of change with respect to its base version. Rendered with the build number as a string in the form `N.M.P-beta.B` e.g. `2.0.1-beta.154`.
Value representing a version which is 'release candidate', i.e. contains only patch-level changes on the base version. Rendered as a string as `N.M.P-rc.B` e.g. `2.0.1-rc.27`.
Value representing a version which is 'released', i.e. is the definitive base version. Rendered with the build number as a string in the form `N.M.P` e.g. `2.0.1`.
Value representing a version which is a build of the current base release. Rendered with the build number as a string in the form `N.M.P+B` e.g. `2.0.1+33`.

BMM_MODEL_ACCESS.name: BMM_MODEL_ACCESS
BMM_MODEL_ACCESS.documentation: Access to BMM models that have been loaded and validated from one or more schema sets.

schema_directories.name: schema_directories
schema_directories.documentation: List of directories where all the schemas loaded here are found.

all_schemas.name: all_schemas
all_schemas.documentation: All schemas found and loaded from `_schema_directory_`. Keyed by `_schema_id_`.

bmm_models.name: bmm_models
bmm_models.documentation: Top-level (root) schemas in use. Table is keyed by `_model_id_`.

matching_bmm_models.name: matching_bmm_models
matching_bmm_models.documentation: `BMM_MODELs` keyed by `_model_id()_` and any shorter form used in calls to

initialise_with_load_list.name: initialise_with_load_list
initialise_with_load_list.documentation: Initialise with a specific schema load list, usually a sub-set of schemas that will be found in a specified directories `_a_schema_dirs_`.

initialise_all.name: initialise_all
initialise_all.documentation: Load all schemas found in a specified directories `_a_schema_dirs_`.

bmm_model.name: bmm_model
bmm_model.documentation: Return ref model containing the model key which is a `_model_id_` or any shorter form e.g. model id minus the version. If a shorter key is used, the `BMM_MODEL` with the highest version will be selected.

has_bmm_model.name: has_bmm_model
has_bmm_model.documentation: True if a model for a `_model_key_` is available. A model key is a `_model_id_` or any shorter form e.g. model id minus the version. If a shorter key is used, the Result s True if a `BMM_MODEL` with any version exists.

BMM_SCHEMA_DESCRIPTOR.name: BMM_SCHEMA_DESCRIPTOR
BMM_SCHEMA_DESCRIPTOR.documentation: Descriptor for a BMM schema. Contains a meta-data table of attributes obtained from a mini-ODIN parse of the  schema file.

meta_data.name: meta_data
meta_data.documentation: Table of `{key, value}` pairs of schema meta-data, keys are string values corresponding to values in `P_BMM_SCHEMA_METADATA_KEY`.

includes.name: includes
includes.documentation: Identifiers of schemas included by this schema.

is_top_level.name: is_top_level
is_top_level.documentation: True if this is a top-level schema, i.e. not included by some other schema.

is_bmm_compatible.name: is_bmm_compatible
is_bmm_compatible.documentation: True if the BMM version found in the schema (or assumed, if none) is compatible with that in this software.

load.name: load
load.documentation: Load schema into in-memory form, i.e. a `P_BMM_SCHEMA` instance, if structurally valid. If successful, `_p_schema_` will be set.

validate_merged.name: validate_merged
validate_merged.documentation: Validate loaded schema and report errors.

validate_includes.name: validate_includes
validate_includes.documentation: Validate includes list for this schema, to see if each mentioned schema exists in `_all_schemas_` list.

create_model.name: create_model
create_model.documentation: Create `schema`, i.e. the `BMM_MODEL` from one `P_BMM_SCHEMA` schema.

BMM_SCHEMA.name: BMM_SCHEMA
BMM_SCHEMA.documentation: Parent of pesistable forms of `BMM_MODEL`. Defines the abstract interface and some common implementation elements for descendants, which provide concrete persistence formats.

bmm_version.name: bmm_version
bmm_version.documentation: Version of BMM model, enabling schema evolution reasoning. Persisted attribute.

includes.name: includes
includes.documentation:
Inclusion list, in the form of a hash of individual include specifications, each of which at least specifies the id of another schema, and may specify a namespace via which types from the included schemas are known in this schema.
Persisted attribute.

bmm_schema.name: bmm_schema
bmm_schema.documentation: Generated by create_bmm_schema from persisted elements.

model_name.name: model_name
model_name.documentation: Name of this model, if this schema is a model root point. Not set for sub-schemas that are not considered models on their own.

schema_name.name: schema_name
schema_name.documentation: Name of model expressed in schema; a 'schema' usually contains all of the packages of one 'model' of a publisher. A publisher with more than one model can have multiple schemas.

validate_created.name: validate_created
validate_created.documentation:
Do some basic validation post initial creation

* check that package structure is regular:
** only top-level packages can have qualified names
** no top-level package name can be a direct parent or child of another (child package must be declared under the parent)
* check that all classes are mentioned in the package structure
* check that all models refer to valid packages

load_finalise.name: load_finalise
load_finalise.documentation:
Finalisation work:

* convert packages to canonical form, i.e. full hierarchy with no packages with names like aa.bb.cc
* set up include processing list

merge.name: merge
merge.documentation: Merge in class and package definitions from `_other_`, except where the current schema already has a definition for the given type or package.

validate.name: validate
validate.documentation: Main validation prior to generation of `_bmm_model_`.

schema_id.name: schema_id
schema_id.documentation:
Identifier of this schema, used for stating inclusions and identifying files. Formed as:

`_rm_publisher_ '_' _schema_name_ '_' _rm_release_`

E.g. `'openehr_rm_ehr_1.0.4'`.

P_BMM_SCHEMA_DESCRIPTOR.name: P_BMM_SCHEMA_DESCRIPTOR
P_BMM_SCHEMA_DESCRIPTOR.documentation: Concrete descendant of `BMM_SCHEMA_DESCRIPTOR` that provides a way to read an ODIN or other similarly encoded P_BMM schema file.

BMM_TYPE.name: BMM_TYPE
BMM_TYPE.documentation: Abstract idea of specifying a type in some context. This is not the same as 'defining' a class. A type specification is essentially a reference of some kind, that defines the type of an attribute, or function result or argument. It may include generic parameters that might or might not be bound. See subtypes.

effective_base_class.name: effective_base_class
effective_base_class.documentation: Effective underlying class for this type, abstracting away any container type.

type_name.name: type_name
type_name.documentation: Formal string form of the type as per UML.

has_subtypes.name: has_subtypes
has_subtypes.documentation: Determine if there are any type substitutions.

subtypes.name: subtypes
subtypes.documentation: List of type substitutions if any available for this type within the current BMM model.

flattened_type_list.name: flattened_type_list
flattened_type_list.documentation: Completely flattened list of type names, flattening out all generic parameters.

type_signature.name: type_signature
type_signature.documentation:
Signature form of the type, which for generics includes generic parameter constrainer types E.g. `Interval<T:Ordered>`.

Generally useful for display purposes.

entity_metatype.name: entity_metatype
entity_metatype.documentation: Meta-type of type is by default assumed to be the meta-type of its `_base_class_`.

BMM_CLASS.name: BMM_CLASS
BMM_CLASS.documentation: Meta-type defining a class definition in an object model.

name.name: name
name.documentation: Name of this class. Note that unlike UML, names of classes are just the root name, even if the class is generic. Use `_type_name()_` to obtain the full type name.

properties.name: properties
properties.documentation: List of attributes defined in this class.

source_schema_id.name: source_schema_id
source_schema_id.documentation: Reference to original source schema defining this class. Useful for UI tools to determine which original schema file to open for a given class for manual editing.

immediate_descendants.name: immediate_descendants
immediate_descendants.documentation: List of references to base classes of immediate inheritance descendants.

is_override.name: is_override
is_override.documentation: True if this definition overrides a class of the same name in an included schema.

all_ancestors.name: all_ancestors
all_ancestors.documentation: List of all inheritance parent class names, recursively.

suppliers.name: suppliers
suppliers.documentation: List of names of immediate supplier classes, including concrete generic parameters, concrete descendants of abstract statically defined types, and inherited suppliers. (Where generics are unconstrained, no class name is added, since logically it would be `Any` and this can always be assumed anyway). This list includes primitive types.

suppliers_non_primitive.name: suppliers_non_primitive
suppliers_non_primitive.documentation: Same as `suppliers` minus primitive types, as defined in input schema.

supplier_closure.name: supplier_closure
supplier_closure.documentation: List of names of all classes in full supplier closure, including concrete generic parameters; (where generics are unconstrained, no class name is added, since logically it would be `Any` and this can always be assumed anyway).  This list includes primitive types.

package_path.name: package_path
package_path.documentation: Fully qualified package name, of form: `package.package`.

class_path.name: class_path
class_path.documentation: Fully qualified class name, of form: `package.package.CLASS` with package path in lower-case and class in original case.

flat_properties.name: flat_properties
flat_properties.documentation: List of all properties due to current and ancestor classes, keyed by property name.

is_primitive.name: is_primitive
is_primitive.documentation: True if this class is designated a primitive type within the overall type system of the schema. Set from schema.

is_abstract.name: is_abstract
is_abstract.documentation: True if this class is abstract in its model. Value provided from an underlying data property set at creation or construction time.

type.name: type
type.documentation: Generate a type object that represents the type of this class.

BMM_PROPERTY.name: BMM_PROPERTY
BMM_PROPERTY.documentation: Model of a property definition within a class definition of an object model. The generic parameter accommodates the possible meta-type variant descendants of `BMM_TYPE`.

name.name: name
name.documentation: Name of this property in the model.

is_mandatory.name: is_mandatory
is_mandatory.documentation: True if this property is mandatory in its class.

is_computed.name: is_computed
is_computed.documentation: True if this property is computed rather than stored in objects of this class.

is_im_runtime.name: is_im_runtime
is_im_runtime.documentation: True if this property is marked with info model `_im_runtime_` property.

is_im_infrastructure.name: is_im_infrastructure
is_im_infrastructure.documentation: True if this property was marked with info model `_im_infrastructure_` flag.

is_synthesised_generic.name: is_synthesised_generic
is_synthesised_generic.documentation: True if this property was synthesised due to generic substitution in an inherited type, or further constraining of a formal generic parameter.

existence.name: existence
existence.documentation: Interval form of `0..1`, `1..1` etc, derived from `_is_mandatory_`.

display_name.name: display_name
display_name.documentation: Name of this property to display in UI.

BMM_CONTAINER_TYPE.name: BMM_CONTAINER_TYPE
BMM_CONTAINER_TYPE.documentation: Type that specifies linear containers with a generic parameter corresponding to the type of contained item, and whose container type is a generic type such as `List<T>`, `Set<T>` etc.

container_class.name: container_class
container_class.documentation: The type of the container. This converts to the `_root_type_` in `BMM_GENERIC_TYPE`.

base_type.name: base_type
base_type.documentation: The target type; this converts to the first parameter in `_generic_parameters_` in `BMM_GENERIC_TYPE`.

flattened_type_list.name: flattened_type_list
flattened_type_list.documentation: Completely flattened list of type names, flattening out all generic parameters.

subtypes.name: subtypes
subtypes.documentation: Result is all permutations of current container type and its descendants with the current `_base_type_` and its descendants.

has_subtypes.name: has_subtypes
has_subtypes.documentation: Determine if there are any type substitutions.

BMM_GENERIC_TYPE.name: BMM_GENERIC_TYPE
BMM_GENERIC_TYPE.documentation: Type reference based on a generic class, e.g. `HashTable <List <Packet>, String>`.

generic_parameters.name: generic_parameters
generic_parameters.documentation: Generic parameters of the `_root_type_` in this type specifier. The order must match the order of the owning class's formal generic parameter declarations.

base_class.name: base_class
base_class.documentation: The target type; this converts to the first parameter in `_generic_parameters_` in `BMM_GENERIC_TYPE`.

type_name.name: type_name
type_name.documentation: Return the full name of the type including generic parameters, e.g. `DV_INTERVAL<T>`, `TABLE<List<THING>,String>`.

type_signature.name: type_signature
type_signature.documentation: Signature form of the type, which for generics includes generic parameter constrainer types E.g. `Interval<T:Ordered>`.

is_abstract.name: is_abstract
is_abstract.documentation: True if `_base_class.is_abstract_` or if any (non-open) parameter type is abstract.

flattened_type_list.name: flattened_type_list
flattened_type_list.documentation: Result is `_base_class.name_` followed by names of all generic parameter type names, which may be open or closed.

subtypes.name: subtypes
subtypes.documentation: Result is the permutation of the base class type and type substitutions of all generic parameters.

has_subtypes.name: has_subtypes
has_subtypes.documentation: True if `_base_class.has_descendants_` or any generic parameter type `_type_substitutions_` is True.

is_partially_closed.name: is_partially_closed
is_partially_closed.documentation: Returns True if there is any substituted generic parameter.

effective_base_class.name: effective_base_class
effective_base_class.documentation: Effective underlying class for this type, abstracting away any container type.

BMM_SIMPLE_TYPE.name: BMM_SIMPLE_TYPE
BMM_SIMPLE_TYPE.documentation: Type reference to a single type i.e. not generic or container type.

effective_base_class.name: effective_base_class
effective_base_class.documentation: Main design class for this type, from which properties etc can be extracted.

BMM_ENUMERATION.name: BMM_ENUMERATION
BMM_ENUMERATION.documentation: Definition of an enumeration type. In the BMM system, an 'enumeration' type is understood as an underlying basic type and a set of named constants of that type. It is designed so that the default type is Integer, and the default constants are numbered 0, 1, ... Optional model elements can be specified to override the values and / or the type.

underlying_type_name.name: underlying_type_name
underlying_type_name.documentation: Name of type any concrete `BMM_ENUMERATION_*` sub-type is based on, i.e. the name of type bound to 'T' in a declared use of this type.

item_names.name: item_names
item_names.documentation: The list of names of the enumeration. If no values are supplied, the integer values 0, 1, 2, ... are assumed.

item_values.name: item_values
item_values.documentation: Optional list of specific values. Must be 1:1 with `_item_names_` list.

underlying_type_name.name: underlying_type_name
underlying_type_name.documentation: Name of type any concrete `BMM_ENUMERATION_*` sub-type is based on.

underlying_type_name.name: underlying_type_name
underlying_type_name.documentation: Name of type any concrete `BMM_ENUMERATION_*` sub-type is based on.

BMM_PACKAGE.name: BMM_PACKAGE
BMM_PACKAGE.documentation: Abstraction of a package as a tree structure whose nodes can contain other packages and classes.

name.name: name
name.documentation: Name of this package. This name may be qualified if it is a top-level package.

classes.name: classes
classes.documentation: Classes listed as being in this package.

root_classes.name: root_classes
root_classes.documentation: Obtain the set of top-level classes in this package, either from this package itself or by recursing into the structure until classes are obtained from child packages. Recurse into each child only far enough to find the first level of classes.

path.name: path
path.documentation: Full path of this package back to root package.

BMM_PARAMETER_TYPE.name: BMM_PARAMETER_TYPE
BMM_PARAMETER_TYPE.documentation: Definition of a generic parameter in a class definition of a generic type.

name.name: name
name.documentation: Name of the parameter, e.g. 'T' etc. The name is limited to 1 character and upper-case.

type_constraint.name: type_constraint
type_constraint.documentation: Optional conformance constraint that must be another valid class name.

inheritance_precursor.name: inheritance_precursor
inheritance_precursor.documentation: If set, is the corresponding generic parameter definition in an ancestor class.

effective_conforms_to_type.name: effective_conforms_to_type
effective_conforms_to_type.documentation: Generate ultimate conformance type, which is either `_flattened_conforms_to_type_` or if not set, `Any`.

type_signature.name: type_signature
type_signature.documentation: Signature form of the open type, including constrainer type if there is one, e.g. `T:Ordered`.

is_primitive.name: is_primitive
is_primitive.documentation: Result = `False` - generic parameters are understood by definition to be non-primitive.

is_abstract.name: is_abstract
is_abstract.documentation: Result = `False` - generic parameters are understood by definition to be non-abstract.

entity_metatype.name: entity_metatype
entity_metatype.documentation: Meta-type of a container type is `Entity_metatype_generic_parameter`.

effective_base_class.name: effective_base_class
effective_base_class.documentation: The effective conformance type of this parameter, or the 'Any' class if none.

flattened_type_list.name: flattened_type_list
flattened_type_list.documentation: Result is either `_flattened_conforms_to_type.flattened_type_list_` or the `Any` type.

BMM_ENTITY.name: BMM_ENTITY
BMM_ENTITY.documentation: Abstract parent of type and class meta-types.

Entity_metatype_simple.name: Entity_metatype_simple
Entity_metatype_simple.documentation: Category of a simple entity, other than enumerated types.

Entity_metatype_generic.name: Entity_metatype_generic
Entity_metatype_generic.documentation: Category of any generic entity that is not a container entity.

Entity_metatype_parameter.name: Entity_metatype_parameter
Entity_metatype_parameter.documentation: Category of a formal generic parameter entity.

Entity_category_modifier_abstract.name: Entity_category_modifier_abstract
Entity_category_modifier_abstract.documentation: Category modifier indicating an entity is formally defined as abstract, i.e. not-directly instantiable within a BMM model.

Entity_category_modifier_primitive.name: Entity_category_modifier_primitive
Entity_category_modifier_primitive.documentation: Category modifier indicating that an entity is considered to be primitive within the type system of a given BMM model. Has no semantic consequences within the BMM.

entity_category.name: entity_category
entity_category.documentation:
Generate a category code-string to use in visualisation, that indicates:

* _entity meta-type_: simple | enumerated | generic | generic_effective | parameter
* _abstract modifier_ - abstract status
* _primitive modifier_ primitive status

The code is structured as:

----
entity_metatype [ '-' abstract_modifier ] [ '-' primitive_modifier ]
----

entity_metatype.name: entity_metatype
entity_metatype.documentation:
Meta-type of entity, which classifies it according to the kind of formal entity it represents from the following:

* simple (non-generic)
* generic
* enumeration
* type parameter
* container type

These meta-types don't exactly match the `BMM_*` types defined in the model, but are useful for displaying models visually.

is_abstract.name: is_abstract
is_abstract.documentation: If true, indicates an abstract class in a BMM model, or a type based on an abstract class, i.e. a type that cannot be directly instantiated.

is_primitive.name: is_primitive
is_primitive.documentation: If True, indicates that the entity in a BMM model is considered to relate to a primitive type set, i.e. be a primitive type, or be a definer of one.

BMM_CONTAINER_PROPERTY.name: BMM_CONTAINER_PROPERTY
BMM_CONTAINER_PROPERTY.documentation: Subtype of `BMM_PROPERTY` that represents a container type based on one of the inbuilt types `List<T>`, `Set<T>`, `Array<T>`.

display_name.name: display_name
display_name.documentation: Name of this property in form `name: ContainerTypeName<>`.

BMM_MODEL.name: BMM_MODEL
BMM_MODEL.documentation: Definition of the root of a BMM model (along with what is inherited from `BMM_SCHEMA_CORE`).

class_definitions.name: class_definitions
class_definitions.documentation: All classes in this schema, keyed by type name.

model_id.name: model_id
model_id.documentation:
Identifier of this model, lower-case, formed from:

`rm_publisher '_' model_name '_' rm_release`

E.g. `'openehr_ehr_1.0.4'`.

class_definition.name: class_definition
class_definition.documentation: Retrieve the class definition corresponding to `_a_type_name_` (which may contain a generic part).

type_definition.name: type_definition
type_definition.documentation: Retrieve the class definition corresponding to `_a_type_name_`. If it contains a generic part, this will be removed if it is a fully open generic name, otherwise it will remain intact, i.e. if it is an effective generic name that identifies a `BMM_GENERIC_CLASS_EFFECTIVE`.

has_class_definition.name: has_class_definition
has_class_definition.documentation: True if `_a_class_name_` has a class definition in the model.

has_type_definition.name: has_type_definition
has_type_definition.documentation: True if `_a_type_name_` is already concretely known in the system, including if it is generic, which may be open, partially open or closed.

enumeration_definition.name: enumeration_definition
enumeration_definition.documentation: Retrieve the enumeration definition corresponding to `_a_type_name_`.

primitive_types.name: primitive_types
primitive_types.documentation: List of keys in `_class_definitions_` of items marked as primitive types.

enumeration_types.name: enumeration_types
enumeration_types.documentation: List of keys in `_class_definitions_` of items that are enumeration types.

property_definition.name: property_definition
property_definition.documentation: Retrieve the property definition for `_a_prop_name_` in flattened class corresponding to `_a_type_name_`.

ms_conformant_property_type.name: ms_conformant_property_type
ms_conformant_property_type.documentation: True if `_a_ms_property_type_` is a valid 'MS' dynamic type for `_a_property_` in BMM type `_a_bmm_type_name_`. 'MS' conformance means 'model-semantic' conformance, which abstracts away container types like `List<>`, `Set<>` etc and compares the dynamic type with the relation target type in the UML sense, i.e. regardless of whether there is single or multiple containment.

property_definition_at_path.name: property_definition_at_path
property_definition_at_path.documentation: Retrieve the property definition for `_a_property_path_` in flattened class corresponding to `_a_type_name_`.

class_definition_at_path.name: class_definition_at_path
class_definition_at_path.documentation: Retrieve the class definition for the class that owns the terminal attribute in `a_prop_path` in flattened class corresponding to `a_type_name`.

all_ancestor_classes.name: all_ancestor_classes
all_ancestor_classes.documentation: Return all ancestor types of `_a_class_name_` up to root class (usually `Any`, `Object` or something similar). Does  not include current class. Returns empty list if none.

is_descendant_of.name: is_descendant_of
is_descendant_of.documentation: True if `_a_class_name_` is a descendant in the model of `_a_parent_class_name_`.

type_conforms_to.name: type_conforms_to
type_conforms_to.documentation:
Check conformance of `_a_desc_type_` to `_an_anc_type_`; the types may be generic, and may contain open generic parameters like 'T' etc. These are replaced with their appropriate constrainer types, or Any during the conformance testing process.

Conformance is found if:

* [base class test] types are non-generic, and either type names are identical, or else `_a_desc_type_` has `_an_anc_type_` in its ancestors;
* both types are generic and pass base class test; number of generic params matches, and each generic parameter type, after 'open parameter' substitution, recursively passes; `_type_name_conforms_to_` test
* descendant type is generic and ancestor type is not, and they pass base classes test.

any_class_definition.name: any_class_definition
any_class_definition.documentation: `BMM_CLASS` instance for the `Any` class. This may be defined in the BMM schema, but if not, a default `Any` class will be generated.

BMM_PACKAGE_CONTAINER.name: BMM_PACKAGE_CONTAINER
BMM_PACKAGE_CONTAINER.documentation: A BMM model component that contains packages and classes.

packages.name: packages
packages.documentation: Child packages; keys all in upper case for guaranteed matching.

do_recursive_packages.name: do_recursive_packages
do_recursive_packages.documentation: Recursively execute `_action_`, which is a procedure taking a `BMM_PACKAGE` argument, on all members of packages.

has_package_path.name: has_package_path
has_package_path.documentation: True if there is a package at the path `_a_path_`; paths are delimited with delimiter `_Package_name_delimiter_`.

BMM_GENERIC_CLASS.name: BMM_GENERIC_CLASS
BMM_GENERIC_CLASS.documentation: Definition of a generic class in an object model.

generic_parameters.name: generic_parameters
generic_parameters.documentation: List of generic parameter definitions, keyed by name of generic parameter; these are defined either directly on this class or by the addition of an ancestor class which is generic.

type.name: type
type.documentation: Generate a fully open `BMM_GENERIC_TYPE` instance that corresponds to this class definition

generic_parameter_conformance_type.name: generic_parameter_conformance_type
generic_parameter_conformance_type.documentation: For a generic class, type to which generic parameter `a_name` conforms e.g. if this class is `Interval <T:Comparable>` then the Result will be the single type `Comparable`. For an unconstrained type `T`, the Result will be `Any`.

BMM_DEFINITIONS.name: BMM_DEFINITIONS
BMM_DEFINITIONS.documentation: Definitions used by all BMM packages.

Bmm_internal_version.name: Bmm_internal_version
Bmm_internal_version.documentation: Current internal version of BMM meta-model, used to determine if a given schema can be processed by a given implementation of the model.

Schema_name_delimiter.name: Schema_name_delimiter
Schema_name_delimiter.documentation: Delimiter used to separate schema id from package path in a fully qualified path.

Package_name_delimiter.name: Package_name_delimiter
Package_name_delimiter.documentation: Delimiter used to separate package names in a package path.

BMM_MODEL_ELEMENT.name: BMM_MODEL_ELEMENT
BMM_MODEL_ELEMENT.documentation: Ancestor class of most BMM model elements.

BMM_UNITARY_TYPE.name: BMM_UNITARY_TYPE
BMM_UNITARY_TYPE.documentation: Parent of meta-types that may be used as the type of any instantiated object that is not a container object.

BMM_INDEXED_CONTAINER_TYPE.name: BMM_INDEXED_CONTAINER_TYPE
BMM_INDEXED_CONTAINER_TYPE.documentation: Type of linear container that indexes the contained items in the manner of a standard Hash table, map or dictionary.

index_type.name: index_type
index_type.documentation: Type of the element index, typically `String` or `Integer`, but may be a numeric type or indeed any type from which a hash value can be derived.

base_class.name: base_class
base_class.documentation: The target type; this converts to the first parameter in `_generic_parameters_` in `BMM_GENERIC_TYPE`.

BMM_SIMPLE_CLASS.name: BMM_SIMPLE_CLASS
BMM_SIMPLE_CLASS.documentation: Definition of a simple class, i.e. a class that has no generic parameters and is 1:1 with the type it generates.

type.name: type
type.documentation: Generate a type object that represents the type of this class. Can only be an instance of `BMM_SIMPLE_TYPE` or a descendant.

BMM_MODEL_METADATA.name: BMM_MODEL_METADATA
BMM_MODEL_METADATA.documentation: Core properties of `BMM_MODEL`, may be used in a serial representation as well, such as `P_BMM_SCHEMA`.

rm_publisher.name: rm_publisher
rm_publisher.documentation: Publisher of model expressed in the schema.

rm_release.name: rm_release
rm_release.documentation: Release of model expressed in the schema as a 3-part numeric, e.g. "3.1.0" .

EXPR_TYPE_DEF.name: EXPR_TYPE_DEF
EXPR_TYPE_DEF.documentation: Ancestor class for type definitions known in the openEHR Expression formalism.

type_name.name: type_name
type_name.documentation: Natural language type name of this type as used in abstract rules syntax variable declarations.

type_anchor.name: type_anchor
type_anchor.documentation: Attribute of the openEHR primitive type (or Any) corresponding to this type definition meta-type.

TYPE_DEF_BOOLEAN.name: TYPE_DEF_BOOLEAN
TYPE_DEF_BOOLEAN.documentation: Rules meta-type representing the primitive type Boolean.

TYPE_DEF_DATE.name: TYPE_DEF_DATE
TYPE_DEF_DATE.documentation: Rules meta-type representing the primitive type Date.

TYPE_DEF_DATE_TIME.name: TYPE_DEF_DATE_TIME
TYPE_DEF_DATE_TIME.documentation: Rules meta-type representing the primitive type Date_time.

TYPE_DEF_DURATION.name: TYPE_DEF_DURATION
TYPE_DEF_DURATION.documentation: Rules meta-type representing the primitive type Duration.

TYPE_DEF_INTEGER.name: TYPE_DEF_INTEGER
TYPE_DEF_INTEGER.documentation: Rules meta-type representing the primitive type Integer.

TYPE_DEF_OBJECT_REF.name: TYPE_DEF_OBJECT_REF
TYPE_DEF_OBJECT_REF.documentation: Rules meta-type representing the type Object_ref, which is assumed to by the type of any non-primitive reference target within a rule.

TYPE_DEF_REAL.name: TYPE_DEF_REAL
TYPE_DEF_REAL.documentation: Rules meta-type representing the primitive type Real.

TYPE_DEF_STRING.name: TYPE_DEF_STRING
TYPE_DEF_STRING.documentation: Rules meta-type representing the primitive type String.

TYPE_DEF_TERMINOLOGY_CODE.name: TYPE_DEF_TERMINOLOGY_CODE
TYPE_DEF_TERMINOLOGY_CODE.documentation: Rules meta-type representing the primitive type Terminology_code.

TYPE_DEF_TIME.name: TYPE_DEF_TIME
TYPE_DEF_TIME.documentation: Rules meta-type representing the primitive type Time.

TYPE_DEF_URI.name: TYPE_DEF_URI
TYPE_DEF_URI.documentation: Rules meta-type representing the primitive type Uri.

ASSERTION.name: ASSERTION
ASSERTION.documentation: Meta-type for a first order predicate logic expression with a Boolean result.

tag.name: tag
tag.documentation: Expression tag, used for differentiating multiple assertions.

string_expression.name: string_expression
string_expression.documentation: String form of expression, in case an expression evaluator taking String expressions is used for evaluation.

ASSIGNMENT.name: ASSIGNMENT
ASSIGNMENT.documentation: Meta-type representing the assignment statement, which associates a named variable with an expression, and produces no value.

target.name: target
target.documentation: The target variable on the notional left-hand side of this assignment.

source.name: source
source.documentation: Source right hand side) of the assignment.

EXPR_FUNCTION_CALL.name: EXPR_FUNCTION_CALL
EXPR_FUNCTION_CALL.documentation: Node representing a function call with 0 or more arguments.

arguments.name: arguments
arguments.documentation: Arguments of this function, which can be from 0 to any number. Functions with no arguments are typically used to represent real world varying values like 'current time' and so on.

EXPRESSION.name: EXPRESSION
EXPRESSION.documentation: Abstract parent of all expression meta-types.

type.name: type
type.documentation: The primitive type of this node, which must be determined by redefinitions in concrete classes.

EXPR_LEAF.name: EXPR_LEAF

item.name: item
item.documentation: The reference item from which the value of this node can be computed.

EXPR_LITERAL.name: EXPR_LITERAL
EXPR_LITERAL.documentation: Literal value expression tree leaf item. This can represent a literal value of any primitive type included in the `PRIMITIVE_TYPE` enumeration.

item.name: item
item.documentation: A statically set constant value of a primitive type.

precedence_overridden.name: precedence_overridden
precedence_overridden.documentation: True if the natural precedence of operators is overridden in the expression represented by this node of the expression tree. If True, parentheses should be introduced around the totality of the syntax expression corresponding to this operator node and its operands.

symbol.name: symbol
symbol.documentation: The symbol actually used in the rule, or intended to be used for serialisation. Must be a member of `operator_def.symbols`.

EXPR_VARIABLE_REF.name: EXPR_VARIABLE_REF
EXPR_VARIABLE_REF.documentation: Expression tree leaf item representing a reference to a declared variable.

EXTERNAL_QUERY.name: EXTERNAL_QUERY
EXTERNAL_QUERY.documentation: Definition of a variable whose value is derived from a query run on a data context in the operational environment. Typical uses of this kind of variable are to obtain values like the patient date of birth, sex, weight, and so on. It could also be used to obtain items from a knowledge context, such as a drug database.

context.name: context
context.documentation:
Optional name of context. This allows a basic separation of query types to be done in more sophisticated environments. Possible values might be “patient”, “medications” and so on.
Not yet standardised.

query_id.name: query_id
query_id.documentation:
Identifier of query in the external context, e.g. “date_of_birth”.
Not yet standardised.

query_args.name: query_args
query_args.documentation:
Optional arguments to query.
Not yet standardised.

EXPR_VALUE.name: EXPR_VALUE
EXPR_VALUE.documentation: Any kind of statement element that can be evaluated. The type will either be supplied in descendant types or else will be inferred by an assignment statement linked to a typed variable.

value.name: value
value.documentation: The computed value of this node as a result of the nodes below it, for operator nodes, or else statically set or otherwise derived values.

STATEMENT_SET.name: STATEMENT_SET
STATEMENT_SET.documentation: A container for a specific set of statements intended to be used together.

statement.name: statement
statement.documentation: The member statements of this statement set.

name.name: name
name.documentation: Optional name of this rule set.

execution_result.name: execution_result
execution_result.documentation: Execution result of the whole rule set. Determined by the and-ing of result values of Assertions in the rule set.

STATEMENT.name: STATEMENT
STATEMENT.documentation: Meta-type for the notion of statement, which is a non-value-returning entity.

VARIABLE_DECLARATION.name: VARIABLE_DECLARATION
VARIABLE_DECLARATION.documentation: Meta-type for the declaration of a named variable that can be used in an expression.

type.name: type
type.documentation: Primitive type of the variable, enabling its use to be type-checked in expressions.

OPERATOR_DEF.name: OPERATOR_DEF
OPERATOR_DEF.documentation: Abstract definition of an operator for use in the Rules evaluator.

identifier.name: identifier
identifier.documentation: Unique identifier of this operator within the rules system.

EXPR_VALUE_REF.name: EXPR_VALUE_REF
EXPR_VALUE_REF.documentation: Path-based reference to a value in a data structure.

EXPR_FOR_ALL.name: EXPR_FOR_ALL
EXPR_FOR_ALL.documentation: Universal quantification operator, usually known as `for_all`, whose operand is a collection of items referenced by an `EXPR_VALUE_REF`. The `_condition_` attribute represents an assertion that is applied to every member of the collection at runtime to determine the result.

condition.name: condition
condition.documentation: Boolean condition that returns True or False when applied to a member of the operand of a `for_all` operator.

operand.name: operand
operand.documentation: Reference to collection / container object to which the `for_all` operator is to be applied.

IDate.name: IDate
IDate.documentation: Interface of an absolute point in time, as measured on the Gregorian calendar, and specified only to the day.

as_string.name: as_string
as_string.documentation: ISO8601 string for date/time, in format `YYYYMMDDThhmmss[,sss][Z | ±hh[mm]]` or in extended format `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss[,sss][Z | ±hh[mm]]` or a partial variant; see valid_iso8601_date_time() below.

IDate_time.name: IDate_time
IDate_time.documentation: Defines the interface of an absolute point in time, specified to fractional seconds.

IDuration.name: IDuration
IDuration.documentation: Defines the interface of a period of time corresponding to a difference between two timepoints.

hour.name: hour
hour.documentation: Hour part of timezone - in the range 00 - 13.

minute.name: minute
minute.documentation: Minute part of timezone. Generally 00 or 30.

sign.name: sign
sign.documentation: Direction of timezone expresssed as +1 or -1.

invariants.Min_hour_valid: sign = -1 implies hour > 0 and hour <= Min_timezone_hour
invariants.Max_hour_valid: sign = 1 implies hour > 0 and hour <= Max_timezone_hour

ITime.name: ITime
ITime.documentation: Defines the interface of an absolute point in time from an origin usually interpreted as meaning the start of the current day, specified to fractional seconds.

Time_Definitions.name: Time_Definitions
Time_Definitions.documentation: Definitions for date/time classes. Note that the timezone limits are set by where the international dateline is. Thus, time in New Zealand is quoted using +12:00, not -12:00.

Nominal_days_in_month.name: Nominal_days_in_month
Nominal_days_in_month.documentation: Used for conversions of durations containing months to days and / or seconds.

Nominal_days_in_year.name: Nominal_days_in_year
Nominal_days_in_year.documentation: Used for conversions of durations containing years to days and / or seconds.

Min_timezone_hour.name: Min_timezone_hour
Min_timezone_hour.documentation: Minimum hour value of a timezone (note that the -ve sign is supplied in the ISO8601_TIMEZONE class).

valid_day.name: valid_day
valid_day.documentation: True if d >= 1 and d <= days_in_month (m, y)

post_conditions.Post: Result = d >= 1 and d <= days_in_month (m, y)

valid_hour.name: valid_hour
valid_hour.documentation: True if (h >= 0 and h < Hours_in_day) or (h = Hours_in_day and m = 0 and s = 0)

post_conditions.Post: Result = (h >= 0 and h < Hours_in_day) or (h = Hours_in_day and m = 0 and s = 0)

valid_minute.name: valid_minute
valid_minute.documentation: True if m >= 0 and m < Minutes_in_hour.

valid_second.name: valid_second
valid_second.documentation: True if s >= 0 and s < Seconds_in_minute .

Timezone.name: Timezone
Timezone.documentation: Assumed built-in primitive type representing a timezone.

Aggregate.name: Aggregate
Aggregate.documentation: Abstract ancestor of container types whose items are addressable in some way.

has.name: has
has.documentation: Test for membership of a value.

TUPLE.name: TUPLE
TUPLE.documentation: Parent type of all TUPLE types.

FUNCTION.name: FUNCTION
FUNCTION.documentation: Type representing a function with a return type and 0 or more arguments represented as a TUPLE.

TUPLE1.name: TUPLE1
TUPLE1.documentation: A Tuple type used, among other things, for representing a single typed argument within a Routine signature.

TUPLE2.name: TUPLE2
TUPLE2.documentation: A Tuple type used, among other things, for representing two typed arguments within a Routine signature.

ROUTINE.name: ROUTINE
ROUTINE.documentation: Type representing a function with a return type and 0 or more arguments represented as a TUPLE.

PROCEDURE.name: PROCEDURE
PROCEDURE.documentation: Type representing a procedure with 0 or more arguments represented as a TUPLE.

Iso8601_timezone.name: Iso8601_timezone
Iso8601_timezone.documentation: Represents a timezone as used in ISO 8601.

is_partial.name: is_partial
is_partial.documentation: True if this time zone is partial, i.e. if minutes is missing.

is_extended.name: is_extended
is_extended.documentation: True if this time-zone uses ‘:’ separators.

as_string.name: as_string
as_string.documentation:
ISO8601 timezone string, in format:

* `Z | ±hh[mm]`

where:

* `hh` is "00" - "23" (0-filled to two digits)
* `mm` is "00" - "59" (0-filled to two digits)
* `Z` is a literal meaning UTC (modern replacement for GMT), i.e. timezone `+0000`

is_gmt.name: is_gmt
is_gmt.documentation: True if timezone is UTC, i.e. +0000.

invariants.Min_hour_valid: sign = -1 implies hour > 0 and hour <= Min_timezone_hour
invariants.Max_hour_valid: sign = 1 implies hour > 0 and hour <= Max_timezone_hour

Proper_interval.name: Proper_interval
Proper_interval.documentation: Type representing a 'proper' Interval, i.e. any two-sided or one-sided interval.

Point_interval.name: Point_interval
Point_interval.documentation: Type representing an Interval that happens to be a point value. Provides an efficient representation that is substitutable for Interval<T> where needed.

lower_included.name: lower_included
lower_included.documentation: lower boundary value included in range if not lower_unbounded.

upper_included.name: upper_included
upper_included.documentation: upper boundary value included in range if not upper_unbounded.

AUTHORED_RESOURCE.name: AUTHORED_RESOURCE
AUTHORED_RESOURCE.documentation: Abstract idea of an online resource created by a human author.

original_language.name: original_language
original_language.documentation: Language in which this resource was initially authored. Although there is no language primacy of resources overall, the language of original authoring is required to ensure natural language translations can preserve quality. Language is relevant in both the description and ontology sections.

is_controlled.name: is_controlled
is_controlled.documentation: True if this resource is under any kind of change control (even file copying), in which case revision history is created.

description.name: description
description.documentation: Description and lifecycle information of the resource.

uid.name: uid
uid.documentation: Unique identifier of the family of archetypes having the same interface identifier (same major version).

annotations.name: annotations
annotations.documentation: Annotations on individual items within the resource, keyed by path. The inner table takes the form of a Hash table of String values keyed by String tags.

translations.name: translations
translations.documentation: List of details for each natural translation made of this resource, keyed by language. For each translation listed here, there must be corresponding sections in all language-dependent parts of the resource. The original_language does not appear in this list.

current_revision.name: current_revision
current_revision.documentation: Most recent revision in revision_history if is_controlled else  (uncontrolled) .

languages_available.name: languages_available
languages_available.documentation: Total list of languages available in this resource, derived from original_language and translations.

invariants.Current_revision_valid: (current_revision /= Void and not is_controlled) implies current_revision.is_equal ("(uncontrolled)")
invariants.Translations_valid: translations /= Void implies (not translations.is_empty and not translations.has (orginal_language.code_string))

RESOURCE_DESCRIPTION.name: RESOURCE_DESCRIPTION
RESOURCE_DESCRIPTION.documentation: Defines the descriptive meta-data of a resource.

original_author.name: original_author
original_author.documentation: Original author of this resource, with all relevant details, including organisation.

original_namespace.name: original_namespace
original_namespace.documentation: Namespace of original author's organisation, in reverse internet form, if applicable.

original_publisher.name: original_publisher
original_publisher.documentation: Plain text name of organisation that originally published this artefact, if any.

other_contributors.name: other_contributors
other_contributors.documentation: Other contributors to the resource, each listed in "name <email>"  form.

lifecycle_state.name: lifecycle_state
lifecycle_state.documentation: Lifecycle state of the resource, typically including states such as: initial, in_development, in_review, published, superseded, obsolete.

custodian_namespace.name: custodian_namespace
custodian_namespace.documentation: Namespace in reverse internet id form, of current custodian organisation.

custodian_organisation.name: custodian_organisation
custodian_organisation.documentation: Plain text name of current custodian organisation.

copyright.name: copyright
copyright.documentation: Optional copyright statement for the resource as a knowledge resource.

licence.name: licence
licence.documentation: Licence of current artefact, in format "short licence name <URL of licence>", e.g. "Apache 2.0 License <http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.html>"

ip_acknowledgements.name: ip_acknowledgements
ip_acknowledgements.documentation:
List of acknowledgements of other IP directly referenced in this archetype, typically terminology codes, ontology ids etc. Recommended keys are the widely known name or namespace for the IP source, as shown in the following example:

----
ip_acknowledgements = <
    ["loinc"] = <"This content from LOINC® is copyright © 1995 Regenstrief Institute, Inc. and the LOINC Committee, and available at no cost under the license at http://loinc.org/terms-of-use">
    ["snomedct"] = <"Content from SNOMED CT® is copyright © 2007 IHTSDO <ihtsdo.org>">
>
----

references.name: references
references.documentation: List of references of material on which this artefact is based, as a keyed list of strings. The keys should be in a standard citation format.

resource_package_uri.name: resource_package_uri
resource_package_uri.documentation: URI of package to which this resource belongs.

conversion_details.name: conversion_details
conversion_details.documentation:
Details related to conversion process that generated this model from an original, if relevant, as a list of name/value pairs. Typical example with recommended tags:

----
conversion_details = <
    ["source_model"] = <"CEM model xyz <http://location.in.clinicalelementmodels.com>">
    ["tool"] = <"cem2adl v6.3.0">
    ["time"] = <"2014-11-03T09:05:00">
>
----

other_details.name: other_details
other_details.documentation: Additional non language-sensitive resource meta-data, as a list of name/value pairs.

details.name: details
details.documentation: Details of all parts of resource description that are natural language-dependent, keyed by language code.

TRANSLATION_DETAILS.name: TRANSLATION_DETAILS
TRANSLATION_DETAILS.documentation: Class providing details of a natural language translation.

language.name: language
language.documentation: Language of the translation, coded using ISO 639-1 (2 character) language codes.

author.name: author
author.documentation: Translator name and other demographic details.

accreditaton.name: accreditaton
accreditaton.documentation: Accreditation of translator, usually a national translator's registration or association membership id.

version_last_translated.name: version_last_translated
version_last_translated.documentation: Version of this resource last time it was translated into the language represented by this TRANSLATION_DETAILS object.

RESOURCE_DESCRIPTION_ITEM.name: RESOURCE_DESCRIPTION_ITEM
RESOURCE_DESCRIPTION_ITEM.documentation: Language-specific detail of resource description. When a resource is translated for use in another language environment, each `RESOURCE_DESCRIPTION_ITEM` needs to be copied and translated into the new language.

language.name: language
language.documentation: The localised language in which the items in this description item are written. Coded using ISO 639-1 (2 character) language codes.

keywords.name: keywords
keywords.documentation: Keywords which characterise this resource, used e.g. for indexing and searching.

use.name: use
use.documentation: Description of the uses of the resource, i.e. contexts in which it could be used.

misuse.name: misuse
misuse.documentation: Description of any misuses of the resource, i.e. contexts in which it should not be used.

original_resource_uri.name: original_resource_uri
original_resource_uri.documentation: URIs of original clinical document(s) or description of which resource is a formalisation, in the language of this description item; keyed by meaning.

other_details.name: other_details
other_details.documentation: Additional language-sensitive resource metadata, as a list of name/value pairs.

RESOURCE_ANNOTATIONS.name: RESOURCE_ANNOTATIONS
RESOURCE_ANNOTATIONS.documentation:
Object representing annotations on an archetype. These can be of various forms, with a documentation form defined so far, which has a multi-level tabular structure [ [ [String value, String key], path key], language key]. Example instance, showing the documentation structure.

--------
    documentation = <
        ["en"] = <
           ["/data[id2]"] = <
               ["ui"] = <"passthrough">
           >
           ["/data[id2]/items[id3]"] = <
               ["design note"] = <"this is a design note on Statement">
               ["requirements note"] = <"this is a requirements note on Statement">
               ["medline ref"] = <"this is a medline ref on Statement">
           >
        >
    >
--------
